title: Stardew Valley
edition_pinned: Stardew Valley 1.0 single-player farm loop (ConcernedApe / Eric Barone; 26 February 2016) — day energy, seasons, Community Center. Multiplayer (1.3) and 1.5/1.6 content are extensions, not the pin.
moat: A shared daily energy+time budget forces tradeoffs across farm, mine, forage, and fish, while social goals compete on the day clock.

loop_skeleton.description: Wake with full energy, spend the day on farm work and town/mine activities that drain energy and/or clock time, sleep to advance the calendar, and turn seasonal goods into Community Center bundles that unlock map systems.

depth_mechanisms.name: energy-time day budget
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Most tools and actions spend energy; the clock advances toward sleep. Exhaustion and pass-out penalize the next day. Farm, mine, forage, and fish compete for energy and clock; social activities consume time on the shared day clock but not energy.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If energy is infinite and the day never forces sleep, multi-activity tradeoffs vanish and second-session planning (what to plant vs mine) becomes pure checklist execution.

depth_mechanisms.name: season crop window
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Crops are legal only in listed seasons and need daily water until harvest; season rollover kills out-of-season plants. The calendar is a fixed constraint on farm plans.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If crops grow in all seasons without calendar loss, first-year farm planning stops treating season transitions as decision points.

depth_mechanisms.name: community center bundle graph
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Bundles demand sets of seasonal and activity-gated items; completing rooms unlocks systems such as the greenhouse, minecarts, and bridge repair. An alternate Joja cash path exists, but the design-canonical long goal is bundle completion.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If bundles are removed and only gold income remains as long goal, second-session return motives shift to money optimization and multi-skill collection drops.

feel_signature: Energy-using tool actions spend energy on a shared day clock (scythes, weapons, and pans are exceptions); success is a bed-save with crops watered and a bundle slot filled, not a boss HP bar.
second_session_answer: Players return to water crops, hit the next mine floors, and finish the next bundle items before season end — goals that persist on the farm calendar.
packaging_lessons: Inference: ship day energy, seasons, and one long collection goal (bundles) before marriage or multiplayer.
Make cooking a utility bonus path rather than pure profit so optimization does not erase exploration (Barone design intent).
anti_lessons: Do not copy a farm sim as infinite free time with no shared budget — without energy/day pressure the loop is a spreadsheet.
Do not require spreadsheets for basic progress; if the only viable play is external optimizers, the day budget failed as a teaching system.

system_bom.note: day energy cycle is the master loop that forces tradeoffs before sleep advances the calendar
crop, animal, and forage sales fund tools and buildings that change tomorrow's work rate
machines and cooking convert raw goods into higher-value or buff items that feed the next day plan
limited slots force what to carry into mines versus what to leave for farm work
bundles and help-wanted boards structure medium goals that compete with pure gold farming
farming, mining, foraging, fishing, and combat levels from use and unlock tools and recipes that expand the day plan
farm layout and calendar must persist so multi-day crop and bundle plans remain intact across sessions

citations.claim: Edition pin: Stardew Valley 1.0 released 26 February 2016 as a single-player farm sim; multiplayer arrived later.
Moat and energy-time day budget: tool actions drain energy; depleted bar forces bed; next day repeats tasks — described as the core loop. Social interaction is not claimed as an energy cost.
Energy: tools spend energy except scythes, weapons, and pans; running out of energy or staying up too late causes pass-out with gold and energy penalties.
Season crop window: crops are season-locked; planting and harvest windows are calendar-gated.
Season rollover kills out-of-season crops; dead plants must be cleared.
Community center bundle graph: bundles unlock areas and systems; Community Center vs Joja as long structure.
Bundle rooms unlock greenhouse, minecarts, bridge, and other map systems.
Joja cash alternate path replaces Community Center restoration with paid community development.
Feel signature three-phase handoff (short goals, long goals, discovery) via mines and bundles.
Feel signature energy and day clock as the shared budget for energy-using tool work (not every tool swing).
Second-session answer: multi-day crops, mine floors, and bundles persist as calendar goals.
Play-loop BOM: day energy cycle forces activity tradeoffs before sleep; social goals compete on time, not energy.
Economy BOM: selling goods funds tools, buildings, and upgrades that change work rate.
Crafting BOM: artisan machines convert crops into higher-value goods; cooking grants buffs.
Cooking utility path and Barone response to pure profit optimizers (design intent).
Inventory BOM: limited inventory slots constrain what is carried into mines versus farm tasks.
Quests BOM: Help Wanted board quests structure medium gold and item goals.
Skills BOM: five skills level from use; proficiency reduces energy on affected tools; scythes, weapons, and pans are unaffected (no energy cost).
Save-state BOM: farm layout, crops, and calendar date persist in the save file across sessions.
Packaging lesson 1 (labeled Inference): energy, seasons, and bundles are the core long loop before marriage or multiplayer extensions.
Packaging lesson 2: cooking as utility/buff path rather than pure profit.
Anti-lesson infinite free time: energy and day length create the shared budget.
Anti-lesson spreadsheet-only play: Barone design notes reject pure profit cooking as the intended path.
